summary: This is a high-quality test suite that comprehensively covers all task requirements. Every major feature has dedicated tests: queue creation/traversal, exponential backoff calculation and application, dead-letter queue accumulation, progress tracking (all three counters), summary generation, and full pipeline integration. TDD cycle annotations are unusually clear and instructive. Mock design via injectable scriptblock parameters is elegant and keeps tests fully independent of real I/O or timing. Edge cases are well-represented: empty queue, zero-total division guard, MaxRetries=0 and MaxRetries=1 boundary values, delay cap (MaxDelayMs), queue exhaustion returning null, mixed success/failure items, and progress callback invocation count and ordering. Deductions: (1) A handful of tests directly mutate tracker fields (e.g., `$tracker.Processed = [int]7`) rather than going through the public Update-Progress interface, which couples tests to internal structure. (2) No test covers null or non-string queue items, which would exercise strict-mode type constraints meaningfully. (3) No test verifies behavior when the ProgressCallback itself throws, leaving a potential unhandled-error path untested. (4) The 'Retrying' counter is tested in isolation but never verified to increment correctly during a live Invoke-ProcessingPipeline run. Despite these gaps, the suite would reliably catch most regressions and represents solid TDD discipline.
